terms.definition: A server-side proposal to merge one branch into another; bundles the diff, description, CI status, and review thread; called Merge Request on GitLab/Bitbucket.
GitLab/Bitbucket name for what GitHub calls a Pull Request — same concept.
The practice of inspecting another engineer's diff before it lands, providing feedback and (usually) an approval that gates the merge.
A PR explicitly marked as work-in-progress; CI runs but reviewers aren't auto-requested. Flip to 'ready for review' when ready.
Server-side rule restricting who can push to a branch and what checks must pass (required PRs, reviews, CI, no force-push).
File (usually `.github/CODEOWNERS`) mapping paths to teams/individuals; the platform auto-requests reviews from owners on matching PRs.
CI jobs (tests, lint, type-check, security scan) that must pass before the merge button is enabled, configured under branch protection.
Merge button option that collapses all PR commits into a single new commit on the target; cleanest log, loses per-commit granularity.
Merge button option that replays each PR commit linearly onto the target with new SHAs; preserves meaningful commit series.
GitHub/GitLab inline code-suggestion that the author can apply with one click; ideal for small fixes (typos, renames).
